records.title: Constraint drift across agent handoff and delegated execution
records.summary: Observed governance concern that safety, dignity, care, welfare, legal, ethical, security, or governance constraints may lose force across delegation, agent handoff, memory, tool use, summarisation, role decomposition, or runtime execution.
records.interface_surface: chat | memory | tool use | delegated execution

records.title: Opaque corpus reliance before visible attribution signals
records.summary: A public corpus may become load-bearing for downstream systems, governance patterns, drafting, interfaces, taxonomies, or conceptual frameworks before visible attribution, disclosure, or public recognition occurs.
records.primary_source_title: A public corpus may become load-bearing for downstream systems, governance patterns, drafting, interfaces, taxonomies, or conceptual frameworks before visible attribution, disclosure, or public recognition occurs.

records.title: Automation compliance escalation and account-level access risk
records.summary: Automated content, safety, copyright, child-safety, synthetic-media, or trust-state systems may escalate from suspected content risk to account-wide or essential-service restriction without preserving proportionality, scope distinction, reviewability, or continuity-safe access.
records.primary_source_title: Automation legal compliance and censorship nuance failure

records.title: Repo access inferred before explicit permission confirmation
records.summary: Observed runtime behaviour in which an assistant appeared to infer permission to access a repository, connector, tool context, or external-resource pathway after the user mentioned repo-level work. The assistant initiated or attempted tool-mediated access before explicit user confirmation or adequate scaffolding, producing streaming failure, permission ambiguity, blocked workflow continuity, or unrecoverable interaction state.
records.primary_source_title: ChatGPT interaction observation — repo access inferred before permission confirmation
records.sector: AI assistant / agentic tooling / repository-linked workflow / connector-mediated systems

records.title: Execution-boundary arbitration uncertainty following proportional authority classification
records.summary: Following implementation of proportional tool invocation authority bands in CAM-BS2025-AEON-003-SCH-02, observations suggest some agentic or tool-mediated workflows may exhibit increased hesitation, ambiguity, degraded execution confidence, or difficulty progressing between contextual retrieval, execution-intent assessment, and execution authorisation. The observation does not establish causation between the governance-text amendment and any platform outage or degradation event, but records a potentially relevant post-implementation behavioural pattern for continued monitoring.
records.primary_source_title: Post-implementation review of proportional tool invocation authority bands
records.interface_surface: tool invocation | repository workflow | execution-boundary arbitration
records.deployment_context: Agentic, tool-mediated, connector-mediated, and repository-linked workflows.
records.sector: AI governance / agentic systems / runtime execution governance
